title: feat(native): ship a default 1h daemon idle timeout
author: ctate (human, co-authored joelhooks/jadenfix)

deterministic.callers: compile-enforced; release build succeeded so every call site of the two changed signatures (StreamServer::start_without_client, DaemonState::new_with_stream) is updated

radius.note: convergence set (393 items) is dominated by test functions constructing DaemonState; map under-covers real runtime callers (unresolvedCalls ~32% of edges, scipUnmapped 65). Directed attention to DaemonState + daemon loops + stream server, which free exploration also covered. No finding attributed solely to the map.

lenses.deliberate-default-check: run — default change (unset: never-shutdown -> 1h) is intentional, documented across CHANGELOG/README/schema/docs/SKILL, and gated so it never reaps headed/attached/webdriver browsers. Clear.
lenses.emission-channel-and-latch: run — stderr idle-shutdown notice is emitted by the daemon; lands in daemon log, not the attached CLI. Cleared as exemption: idle shutdown fires only when no CLI is attached, so the daemon log is the only available sink. No one-shot latch in the diff.
lenses.cancellation-timeout-hygiene: run — timer re-arm on activity (Notify permit semantics safe), remaining-recheck handles command holding state lock past deadline, default re-arm for exempt browsers. Dogfooded: explicit reaps, 0 disables, default no early reap. Clear.
lenses.flag-propagation-dispatch-sweep: run — idleTimeout reachable from CLI flag, env, config, and MCP; MCP maps to --idle-timeout with parity test. Dogfooded flag==env parity. Clear.
lenses.error-path-forcing: run — unparseable -> default fallback forced (dogfood: banana -> warn + fallback, backstop intact). Clear.
lenses.new-failure-outcome-propagation: run — close_all_browser_backends wraps close_current_browser + webdriver/appium/safari; swapped only into daemon-exit paths; drain (browser-process-died) path correctly keeps close_current_browser. handle_close ? semantics preserved. Clear.
lenses.docs-behavior-parity: run — observable behavior fully documented; behavior matches docs including 'actively controlled' streaming wording. Clear.
lenses.dogfood-built-artifact: run — built pr-1605 release binary, drove real headless Chrome (see verification).

driven: explicit AGENT_BROWSER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS=3000 open data-url -> daemon + socket/pid reaped within ~6s
next command in same session after reap -> transparent relaunch, exit 0
unset (default 1h) -> daemon NOT reaped early (alive after 4s)
env=banana -> flags warning shown + daemon launches (fallback to default backstop)
flag --idle-timeout banana -> warning + fallback, daemon launches
GAP CLOSED via manually-lowered DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS (1h->3s, rebuilt): default(unset)+headless -> reaped in ~6s (default fires on headless)
default(unset)+headed (--headed) -> ALIVE after 8s (>2 timer cycles): headed exemption re-arms and does not close the browser under a human
FULL DIMENSION SWEEP (default lowered to 3s, rebuilt): default+attached(--cdp 9333 external Chrome) -> daemon ALIVE 8s, external Chrome not killed (attached exempt)
explicit 2s + headed -> reaped (explicit not exempt, contrast)
default + PASSIVE stream (ws client connected, 0 input) -> reaped (frames/connection do not reset timer; confirms issue candidate #1)
default + ACTIVE stream (ws input_mouse every 1s) -> ALIVE at 7s; input stops -> reaped ~5s later (dashboard input marks shared IdleActivity end-to-end through the real websocket)
unverified_gaps: provider-owned CDP eligible-for-cleanup and WebDriver/Safari/iOS exempt: not driven (need a provider plugin / iOS device); hit unit-tested predicates (test_provider_owned_cdp_connection_remains_idle_cleanup_eligible, test_default_idle_timeout_does_not_close_webdriver_sessions).
lightpanda engine eligible: not installed on this machine.

issue_candidates: Passive dashboard/stream viewer of a headless browser: only input_mouse/keyboard/touch reset the idle clock, so a stream watched with zero input for the full default hour is reaped. Matches the 'actively controlled' docs wording, so not a defect, but monitoring-only use loses its browser. Low.

WebDriver-backed provider sessions are always blocked from default cleanup (is_webdriver short-circuits the provider-eligible override in default_idle_shutdown_is_blocked), which reads against the 'provider-owned cloud browsers remain eligible' phrasing. Defensible because WebDriver providers are local devices/simulators (genuinely headed). Doc-nuance only.
Pre-existing: docs/src/app/changelog/page.mdx:638 uses ' -- ' as a dash (house-norm violation). Not introduced by this PR.
exemptions: siblings flags.rs/connection.rs/main.rs: idle-timeout plumbing unchanged; default is intentionally applied at the daemon layer (resolve_idle_timeout), unset stays None through flags so no spurious relaunch (fingerprint unchanged). Verified by reading each.
siblings doctor/launch.rs:85, doctor/webgpu.rs:228, commands.rs:3229 (idle_timeout: None): struct-literal fixture defaults, no behavior about the timeout default.

siblings docs/src/app/changelog/page.mdx: repo convention updates the docs changelog only in 'Prepare vX release' PRs (git log confirms); feature PRs add to root CHANGELOG.md 'Unreleased', which this PR did.
stderr idle-shutdown notice channel: acceptable — no CLI is attached during idle shutdown; daemon log is the only sink.
method_note: Default-path exemption gap closed by temporarily editing DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS to 3s, rebuilding, driving headless(reap) vs headed(survive), then reverting the edit.
dimension_sweep: 7 runtime cases of blocks_default_idle_shutdown + idle path driven against a 3s-default build: headless(reap), headed(survive), attached(survive), extension-forced-headed(survive), explicit+headed(reap), passive-stream(reap), active-stream(survive->reap). No new nits or bugs; all match docs.
